serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw whiting is one medium fillet weighing approximately 120 g, suitable for a single adult portion when grilled, steamed, or baked.

nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macros (energy 90 kcal, protein 18.31 g, fat 1.31 g, carbohydrate 0 g, sodium 72 mg, potassium 249 mg, calcium 48 mg, iron 0.34 mg, cholesterol 67 mg) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo. Saturated fat (0.25 g), monounsaturated fat (0.18 g), polyunsaturated fat (0.49 g), trans fat (0 g), water (80.27 g), phosphorus (224 mg), magnesium (28 mg), zinc (0.43 mg), selenium (26.6 mcg), vitamin B12 (1.0 mcg), vitamin B6 (0.29 mg), niacin (1.74 mg), riboflavin (0.08 mg), thiamine (0.07 mg), vitamin D (1.0 mcg) cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for whiting, raw (FDC ID 175177).

gi_source.title: N/A — Whiting, raw contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for raw whiting as it contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. No GI value exists or is meaningful for this food. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. The numeric value 0 is used here as a machine-readable sentinel consistent with the validator requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey the true clinical interpretation. This is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database policy for negligible-carbohydrate foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (72 mg/100 g) with high potassium (249 mg/100 g) yielding a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Raw whiting is well-suited for hypertension management due to its low sodium content (72 mg/100 g) and high potassium (249 mg/100 g), which together support blood pressure regulation consistent with DASH diet principles; patients should avoid adding salt, high-sodium sauces, or processed whiting products during preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and sugar; high protein (18.31 g/100 g) with no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Whiting is an excellent protein source for individuals with type 2 diabetes, providing 18.31 g protein per 100 g with zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, resulting in no glycaemic response; grilling, steaming, or baking are preferred methods, and battered or breaded preparations must be avoided as they introduce high-GI carbohydrates.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.25 g/100 g), beneficial omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (0.49 g/100 g), and lean protein profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Raw whiting supports cardiovascular health through its very low saturated fat content (0.25 g/100 g), presence of omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids, and lean high-quality protein (18.31 g/100 g); regular consumption of lean white fish is consistent with heart-healthy dietary guidelines, and patients should prepare it without deep-frying or high-fat sauces.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA precursors) and very low saturated fat support anti-inflammatory dietary profile
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Whiting provides omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids and high-quality protein that may help reduce joint inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis; steaming, baking, or grilling is preferred to preserve omega-3 content and avoid AGE formation from deep-frying, and supplementing with higher-omega-3 fatty fish (salmon, mackerel) twice weekly is recommended for optimal benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Whiting (Merlangius merlangus) is a lean marine white fish found predominantly in the North Atlantic and European coastal waters. In its raw state, it presents as a boneless fillet with a silvery-blue back and white to light-grey flesh that is delicate, moist, and flaky in texture. It has a mild, slightly sweet flavour that makes it highly versatile for grilling, steaming, baking, poaching, and pan-frying. Nutritionally, raw whiting is an excellent source of high-quality complete protein at 18.31 g per 100 g, with very low total fat (1.31 g) and negligible saturated fat (0.25 g). It contains zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium is low at 72 mg per 100 g and potassium is high at 249 mg per 100 g, supporting cardiovascular and blood pressure health. It provides meaningful amounts of selenium (26.6 mcg), phosphorus (224 mg), vitamin B12 (1.0 mcg), and vitamin D (1.0 mcg). Clinically, whiting is appropriate for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. As a lean white fish, it has lower omega-3 content than fatty fish such as salmon or mackerel, so it is best complemented by regular consumption of oily fish for optimal anti-inflammatory benefit. The delicate texture and mild flavour make it a popular choice for light, healthy meals across Western cuisines.

How many calories in whiting fish?

confidence_reason: Primary macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95. Secondary nutrient values cross-referenced and confirmed against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175177) for raw whiting, which is an exact match. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as whiting is a pure animal protein and fat food with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Sugar_g is naturally zero for the same reason. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as fish contains no dietary fibre. Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for raw whiting. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: retinol activity equivalent data not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for this specific entry. Folate_mcg_dfe set to -1: folate DFE data not reported in HPB Foodo; USDA SR Legacy reports 0 mcg but DFE conversion not confirmed. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo; USDA SR Legacy does not provide a reliable value for this entry. Glycemic index: whiting is a negligible-carbohydrate food (0 g carbohydrate per 100 g); glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The numeric value 0 is stored in glycemic_index as a machine-readable sentinel required by the validator; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey the correct clinical interpretation. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. The rheumatoid_arthritis suitability flag in the input was incorrectly set to suitable=false despite the supporting reason describing whiting as beneficial; this has been corrected to reflect the anti-inflammatory benefit of omega-3 content and lean protein in the health_context section. Ontology: FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are defensible matches based on published taxonomy; SNOMED CT has no applicable food concept for raw whiting.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record for Whiting, raw (F03010042) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1-Q8 scorecard. The sole failure from iteration 2 (q4: glycemic_index must be numeric) has been resolved by setting glycemic_index to 0 as a numeric sentinel, with gi_category not_applicable and comprehensive notes clarifying the clinical interpretation. Primary nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo with cross-referencing to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for extended micronutrients. All zero and -1 values are documented. All three mandatory health conditions are present with specific clinical guidance. Aliases are consistent between flat array and structured object. Ontology anchors are defensible. LLM description exceeds 200 characters and is semantically rich. Western cuisine does not trigger mandatory non-English language requirements under G6.
